text:
“There’s no such thing as failure — only feedback.”
“Embrace the challenges.”
“Your perspective is invaluable.”
“Your voice matters — use it.”

We asked the people scaling Meta to share the career lessons that changed everything for them. 💡

5 Career Tips from the People Scaling Meta ✨
@koolmoebri @aakarsha91 @b_glea @waverville

#MetaCareers #LifeAtMeta #careertips #mentorship

From startup founder & reality TV contestant → Director building AI wearables at Meta. 👓

In this AMA, @hind.hobeika, Director of Product Management, answers your questions about her unique path. She shares why she prioritizes career bets that maximize learning and explains why keeping a close eye on people problems  is the key to building successful AI hardware.

▶️Watch the full AMA to learn how Hind is helping build the future of AI on smart glasses.

#LifeAtMeta #MetaCareers #CareerTips #Leadership #AMA

“I’ve been here for seven years and every day, I walk into the building and feel like it’s the first day I’m coming to work. There’s so much I don’t know on a daily basis, which keeps me so alive.” – Dharmesh Jani, Open Systems Hardware Lead

Want to grow your skills while solving unique challenges across the AI stack? Discover why Steve Mills, mechanical engineer, and Dharmesh Jani (DJ), open systems hardware lead, choose to build at Meta.

#LifeAtMeta #AI #Infra #Hardware #Compute"

What does career growth look like for data engineers at Meta? According to @shri_iyer27, senior technical lead director of data governance, it means real product impact, solving complex problems at massive scale and evolving your skills as AI reshapes the field.

“We’ve moved from questioning whether data engineering as a role will even exist with AI, to our current state where data engineers hold the true IP of data and how it’s molded and understood. Every other role relies on our work and our insights to make AI perform. This evolution has been nothing short of transformative.”

Hear how Shridhar has grown his career over a decade at Meta — and why data engineers here have a true seat at the table.

🔗 Link in bio.

#LifeAtMeta #MetaCareers #AI #DataEngineering #CareerGrowth

Ever wonder what it’s like to build technology for a future you can’t yet predict? Meet Steve Mills and Dharmesh Jani (DJ) — two engineers helping to drive infrastructure innovation at Meta forward. With how fast model sizes are growing, Steve and DJ need to develop multiple hardware solutions at once to ensure they’re always prepared for however the technology might change.

Learn how the current pace of innovation is creating a once-in-a-lifetime career opportunity for today’s engineers at Meta. Click here ➡️ https://meta.careers/4l2DvbN

#LifeAtMeta #Infra #AI #Compute

What does a career in AI research look like when impact comes first? Learn from Jamelle Watson-Daniels, a research scientist at Meta Fundamental AI Research (FAIR), as she shares five lessons from her work on algorithmic fairness.

🔗 Link in our Stories

#LifeAtMeta #MetaCareers #AI #Research #CareerTips

Our goal is to be water positive in 2030. 💧

In this AMA, Stefanie Woodward, Water Stewardship Lead at Meta, shares more about our water restoration program to achieve our goal.

These water restoration projects: 
🌍 Support habitat restoration
🚰 Support water supply reliability and quality
💙 Expand access to safe drinking water

Watch the full AMA and read our annual Sustainability report to learn more with the link in our Stories. 

#sustainability

Why is @pelhamvancooten, Data Engineer, still excited to be at Meta after 5+ years? It comes down to two things: the incredibly smart people he gets to work with every day and the opportunity to keep growing. 👨‍💻 

Pelham joined as an intern because he loved Instagram — he was a total power user who wanted to work on something he cared about. But he stayed because of the culture of collaboration and meaningful growth.

From day one, Pelham was trusted with real projects. Not just shadowing or busy work, but actual contributions that mattered. And that encouraged him to grow fast.

The best advice Pelham ever got here? “At Meta, nothing is someone else’s problem.” That mindset changed everything. He started stepping up, filling gaps, and taking on challenges outside his comfort zone. And that’s where the best growth happens.

Five years in, Pelham is still learning something new every day. That’s why he stays. 💙
Curious what your story could look like? Check out careers at Meta: https://meta.careers/4tOZ9EF 

#LifeAtMeta #MetaCareers #CareerOpportunity

When @metzeflorian first began working with neural networks 20 years ago, voice AI meant long wait times and limited, pre-scripted answers.

Today, Florian is a research scientist manager at Meta — designing conversational voice AI experiences for wearables like Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses. As the technology has evolved, so has his work — shifting from simply making systems function to creating intuitive, human-centered experiences, all while navigating wearable-specific constraints like battery life and local compute.

Read Florian’s story to learn how this evolution has pushed him to be more innovative, and why he believes future AI researchers should leave room for career pivots with the link in our Stories. 

#LifeAtMeta #MetaCareers #Wearables #AI #CareerGrowth

For ten years, technical program manager Aditi Modak (@addory18 ) has helped support the future of technology at Meta. From launching Ray-Ban Meta glasses to pioneering AI that brings live video to wearable devices, Aditi’s journey is full of creativity, challenges, and teamwork. 

She credits her success to Meta’s culture — a place where people support one another, share ideas, and celebrate wins together. Aditi believes mentorship is the secret to lasting growth, dedicating time to guide new technologists and help them find their spark. She shared:

“If building world-class products and solving really hard problems with incredibly talented and fun people motivates you, it doesn’t get better than this team.”

Outside of work, her passion for learning takes her on new adventures. Always in search of inspiration, Aditi enjoys exploring hiking trails, sampling global cuisines, and discovering the best local coffee spots whenever she can.

#LifeAtMeta #engineering #innovation #MetaCareers #womenintech

From machine learning PhD → leading the wearable devices portfolio at Meta. 👓

In this AMA, Ming Hua, VP of wearable devices, answers your questions about her journey to leadership. Her advice? Start building, stay curious and don’t wait to have all the answers before you jump in.

▶️Watch the full AMA to learn how Ming brings state-of-the-art consumer hardware to life.

#LifeAtMeta #MetaCareers #CareerTips #Leadership #AMA AI

“Cross-functional collaboration is a core part of how we operate at Meta. I regularly collaborate with the site operations and facilities engineering teams to ensure data centers run safely and efficiently.”

Brian A. (@beerock81) is a critical operations manager helping to ensure the safe operation and maintenance of Meta data centers. He says the level of teamwork has been one of the biggest surprises of working at Meta.

“I can honestly say this is the best job I’ve ever had. The teamwork here is exceptional — everyone is supportive, and you’ll feel welcomed and valued from day one. You’re always challenged in positive ways, and there are plenty of opportunities for growth and development.”

#LifeAtMeta #MetaCareers #Collaboration #CareerGrowth
